stats.title: Statistics
stats.empty: No finished run in the last {days} days — launch an agent on a card and stats will show up here.

support.title: Looking for something more?
support.body: Open an <issue>issue</issue> or reach out on

kanban.title: Kanban Board
kanban.description: Manage your tasks and agent workflows

cardModal.newDescription: Add a new task to the board.
cardModal.title: Title
cardModal.promptPlaceholder: Prompt to run an AI agent on this task...
cardModal.locked: Only draft tasks can be edited. Move this task back to Draft to change it.

assist.blocked: This preset failed its test — fix it or pick another before creating.
assist.devOnly: Only available in the desktop app
assist.generateHint: Draft from the title and description using this agent
assist.generateError: The agent could not generate a result
assist.promptMeta: Write a single, concise agent task prompt (imperative, 2-5 sentences) for a coding-agent task card named "{name}". Context: "{description}". Output ONLY the prompt text the agent should follow — no preamble, no quotes, no markdown.
assist.tagsMeta: Propose 2 to 5 short lowercase keyword tags for a task card named "{name}" described as "{description}". Output ONLY a comma-separated list of tags, nothing else.
assist.needContext: Add a title and a description first so the agent has context
assist.previewQuestion: The agent raised a question — refine the description, then regenerate

bulk.noMovable: No selected tasks can move to that column.
bulk.noLaunchable: No selected tasks can be launched.

columnsMenu.title: Columns
columnsMenu.description: Show, hide, or rename board columns.
columnsMenu.attentionProtected: Columns with tasks needing attention stay visible.

toast.queued: Agent queued — waiting for a free slot.

schedules.title: Patrols
schedules.description: Configure recurring tasks and automated agent runs

form.name: Name
form.descriptionPlaceholder: e.g. Audit dependencies and open PRs for safe updates

schedules.emptyState: No patrols yet. Create one to get started.

dailyBrief.label: Daily brief
dailyBrief.name: Daily brief
dailyBrief.description: Morning summary of overnight repo activity and the board.
dailyBrief.prompt: Summarize what changed in the repo since yesterday: new commits, open PRs, failing checks, and any cards waiting on me. Keep it to a short bulleted brief.

standupPrep.label: Scrum daily prep
standupPrep.name: Scrum daily prep
standupPrep.description: Talking points for the daily standup, ready before the meeting.
standupPrep.prompt: Prepare my daily scrum standup notes in three sections: Yesterday (commits I pushed and cards I moved to Done), Today (cards in In Progress and Todo I plan to work on), Blockers (cards stuck in Waiting Feedback or Awaiting Review, and any failing checks). Keep each point one short line.

weeklyReview.label: Weekly review
weeklyReview.name: Weekly review
weeklyReview.description: End-of-week board review and stale-card cleanup.
weeklyReview.prompt: Review the Kanban board: list cards that haven't moved in over a week, flag anything stuck in Waiting Feedback or Awaiting Review, and propose next steps for each.

depUpdates.label: Dependency updates
depUpdates.name: Dependency updates
depUpdates.description: Check for outdated dependencies and propose bumps.
depUpdates.prompt: Check for outdated dependencies, identify which are safe to bump, and draft the upgrade with a short note on any breaking changes.

testSweep.label: Test sweep
testSweep.name: Test sweep
testSweep.description: Run the test suite and report failures.
testSweep.prompt: Run the full test suite. If anything fails, summarize the failures and the most likely cause for each.

sortMyComputer.label: Sort out my computer
sortMyComputer.name: Sort out my computer
sortMyComputer.description: Let the agent tidy up a folder on your computer — rename, reorganize, and clean up files.
sortMyComputer.prompt: Sort out the folder you are in: scan its contents, group files by type and purpose, suggest a clear folder structure, rename files with consistent naming conventions, and move or delete obvious duplicates and junk.

prReview.label: PR review
prReview.name: PR review
prReview.description: Review open pull requests for issues and summarize what needs attention.
prReview.prompt: Review the currently open pull requests. For each, summarize what it changes, flag correctness, security or style concerns, and tell me which ones are ready to merge and which need work.

findVulns.label: Find vulnerabilities
findVulns.name: Find Vulnerabilities
findVulns.description: Review pull requests for exploitable security issues and flag only validated findings before merge.
findVulns.prompt: Scan the open pull requests and recently changed code for exploitable security vulnerabilities (injection, auth bypass, secret leakage, unsafe deserialization). Report only validated findings with the file, the risk, and a suggested fix.

secretScan.label: Secret scan
secretScan.name: Secret scan
secretScan.description: Scan the repository for committed secrets, tokens and credentials.
secretScan.prompt: Scan the repository for committed secrets: API keys, tokens, passwords, private keys and connection strings. List each match with its file and line, and recommend whether to rotate and how to remove it from history.

incidentTriage.label: Incident triage
incidentTriage.name: Incident triage
incidentTriage.description: Triage new incidents and error spikes, then propose an owner and next step.
incidentTriage.prompt: Check for new incidents and error spikes since the last run. For each, summarize the likely cause from recent commits and logs, propose an owner, and suggest the first remediation step. Keep each incident to a few lines.

errorDigest.label: Error digest
errorDigest.name: Error digest
errorDigest.description: Daily digest of the top errors with frequency and a probable cause.
errorDigest.prompt: Produce a digest of the top errors over the last 24 hours: group by message, show frequency and first/last seen, and for the top five give a probable cause and a suggested fix.

competitorWatch.label: Competitor watch
competitorWatch.name: Competitor watch
competitorWatch.description: Weekly summary of notable competitor releases, pricing and announcements.
competitorWatch.prompt: Summarize notable competitor activity from the past week: product releases, pricing changes, and public announcements. Group by competitor and end with what is most relevant to our roadmap.

agent.blocked: This preset failed its test — fix it or pick another before creating.
agent.devOnly: Only available in the desktop app
agent.generateHint: Draft from the name and description using this agent
agent.generateError: The agent could not generate a result
agent.promptMeta: Write a single, concise agent task prompt (imperative, 2-5 sentences) for a recurring patrol coding-agent run named "{name}". Context: "{description}". Output ONLY the prompt text the agent should follow — no preamble, no quotes, no markdown.
agent.tagsMeta: Propose 2 to 5 short lowercase keyword tags for a patrol named "{name}" described as "{description}". Output ONLY a comma-separated list of tags, nothing else.
agent.needContext: Add a name and a description first so the agent has context
agent.previewQuestion: The agent raised a question — refine the description, then regenerate

schedules.subtitle: Deploy recurring patrols with AI agents triggered by events.
schedules.seeAll: Showing {shown} of {total} — Click to see all patrols
schedules.templatesHeading: Need ideas? Start from a template

harnessGate.title: Install an agent to create a patrol
harnessGate.description: OpenCode must be installed before you can create or use a patrol.
harnessGate.ollamaDescription: Ollama lets you run open-source models on your machine instead of cloud APIs. Install it separately to use local models with OpenCode.

planner.title: Day Planner
planner.description: Plan your day with AI-generated task breakdowns
planner.objectivesPlaceholder: Describe your goals for today in plain language...
planner.noTasks: No tasks generated yet. Describe your objectives and click Generate.
planner.objectivesLabel: What do you want to accomplish today?
planner.createCards: Create {count, plural, one {# Task} other {# Tasks}}

runs.title: Operations
runs.subtitle: Lists all the running agents
runs.needsYouHint: “Needs you” lists the agents where you need to steer the Agent. The idea is to reduce the “Needs you” agents so the Agents can run without you.
runs.archiveHint: “Done” operations are archived automatically every night at midnight ({timezone}).
runs.footer: Showing {shown} of {total} — click to see all
runs.filteredHidden: {hidden, plural, =0 {Filtered} one {# run hidden} other {# runs hidden}}

history.title: History
history.subtitle: Lists the past runs, analyse the agent discussion

agentSession.discussionEmpty: No agent output recorded for this run.
agentSession.notFound: This run could not be found.

logs.title: Logs & History
logs.description: View agent run history and output logs

workingMessages: Hauling crumbs back to the nest…

review.awaitingTitle: This run is awaiting your review
review.questionTitle: The agent is waiting for your input
review.suggestPatrolHint: You requested changes during this run — turn that feedback into an update of the patrol itself.
review.revisePlaceholder: Describe what needs to change, then Request changes — or just Approve…
review.followUpPlaceholder: Reply to the agent — your message relaunches the run…

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Configure agents, preferences, and app behavior

hub.title: Hub
hub.connected: Connected · {count, plural, =0 {no machines online} one {# machine online} other {# machines online}}

connections.title: Connections
connections.description: Backends this board aggregates. Add remote servers; tasks from each are tagged by origin. The local connection is permanent.
connections.invalidUrl: Enter a full URL (http:// or https://).

version.outdatedHint: This server is behind the latest release ({latest}). Re-run the installer to update it.
version.aheadHint: This server is newer than the latest published release ({latest}).

hub.title: Hubs
hub.description: Connect to a hub to gather all of your remote instances under one login. Each instance appears as its own board origin.
hub.loginFailed: Could not sign in to the hub.
hub.pairFailed: Could not create a pairing code.
hub.enrollHint: On the machine to add, run:

localServer.title: Local Server
localServer.description: Run Myra's server on this computer and connect to it directly over localhost — no hub or internet needed.
localServer.notInstalled: Not set up yet — running a temporary server for this session.
localServer.secured: Secured with a local access token
localServer.setupFailed: Could not set up the local server.
localServer.removeFailed: Could not remove the local server.

remote.title: Remote Access
remote.description: Reach this computer's local server from the web app anywhere. Layers your hub on top of the local server, running Myra in the background as a per-user service enrolled to your hub.
remote.reachable: This computer is reachable via {hub} as {label}
remote.localOnly: Local only — this computer isn't reachable remotely.
remote.needHub: Add a hub in the Connections section above first, then come back here.

consent.title: Reach this computer from anywhere?
consent.body: Run Myra in the background so you can reach this computer's agents from the web app anywhere. It installs a per-user background service. You can stop it anytime in Settings.
consent.needHub: Add a hub in Settings first, then enable remote access.

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.description: Plugins installed under ~/.myra-agents/plugins. Switch one off to drop its agents from the picker; changes apply after Save and a restart.
plugins.empty: No plugins installed. Drop one under ~/.myra-agents/plugins and restart.
plugins.inboundUrl: Inbound URL — paste this into the sender (e.g. GitHub webhook).

webhookWarning.title: Webhooks run in the local server
webhookWarning.body: They only fire while Myra is running and stop when you quit the app or shut down. For 24/7 delivery, install the background service or use a remote instance.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.description: Configured integration instances. Run a plugin more than once — each with its own config, secret, triggers, and the machines it's deployed to.
integrations.empty: No integrations yet. Create one to send task updates to Slack, receive webhooks, and more.
integrations.deleteConfirm: Delete the “{label}” integration from every machine?
integrations.deletePartial: Some machines couldn't be updated — try again.

webhookWarning.title: Webhooks run in the local server
webhookWarning.body: They only fire while Myra is running and stop when you quit the app or shut down. For 24/7 delivery, install the background service or use a remote instance.

wizard.subtitle: Configure a plugin instance and deploy it to your machines.
wizard.noPlugins: No webhook plugins installed. Drop one under ~/.myra-agents/plugins.
wizard.label: Label
wizard.eventsHint: Task statuses that fire this integration's outbound webhook.
wizard.templateHint: Mustache-lite — use card.title and card.status placeholders. Leave blank for the plugin default.
wizard.pickMachines: Pick which of your linked machines run this integration.
wizard.inboundUrl: Inbound URL (per machine) — paste into the sender
wizard.secretNote: Secrets are pushed to each selected machine's keychain now. Keychains can't be copied between machines, so a machine added later will prompt for the secret again.
wizard.deployPartial: Deployed, but {count} machine(s) failed — check connections and retry.

sync.title: Encrypted sync
sync.description: Sync your integrations and their secrets across your devices, end-to-end encrypted. The cloud never sees your secrets or keys.
sync.unavailable: Sync needs the Myra cloud. Configure a hub URL to enable it.
sync.signInRequired: Sign in to set up encrypted sync across your devices.
sync.setUpHint: Generates this device's key and a recovery code, then encrypts a vault key only your devices can open.
sync.joinHint: Enter the recovery code from your first device to decrypt the vault here — works even if your other devices are offline.
sync.lockedHint: This device is enrolled but locked. Unlock to sync.
sync.e2eBody: Integrations and secrets are encrypted on this device before they reach the cloud. The hub only relays ciphertext.
sync.revokeConfirm: Revoke “{label}”? This rotates the vault key so the removed device can't read future changes — you'll get a new recovery code.
sync.recoveryWarning: This is shown once. It's the only way to recover your vault from a new device if all your other devices are offline. Store it somewhere safe — we can't show it again.
sync.recoveryAck: I've saved my recovery code somewhere safe.

agents.title: Agent Presets
agents.description: Configure which AI agents can run your tasks
agents.advancedArgsHint: Must contain '{prompt}'. Use to customize how the prompt is passed to the binary.
agents.name: Name
agents.maxConcurrentHint: How many agents may run at once. 0 = unlimited; extra launches are queued.
agents.keepAwakeHint: Prevents the machine from sleeping while at least one agent is running, so a local-model session isn't cut off.
agents.checkingInstall: Checking whether the agent is installed…
agents.testTooltip: Send a "hello" prompt to verify the agent is reachable
agents.removeConfirmDescription: This will permanently remove the preset. This action cannot be undone.
agents.testAndSaveTooltip: Test the agent, then save if it responds

embedded.description: Embedded agent — no install, no CLI. Runs on the Myra hub, or with your own key.
embedded.apiKeyPlaceholder: sk-or-…  — leave empty to use the Myra hub
embedded.modelHint: Leave empty or "auto" to let Myra pick a free model.
embedded.ollamaModelHint: The local Ollama model tag to run (e.g. qwen2.5-coder). Requires Ollama installed.

preferences.title: Preferences
preferences.themeDescription: Theme changes apply immediately and are stored for your next launch.
preferences.homeFolderHint: Base folder the patrol folder picker browses. Defaults to your user home folder.
preferences.timezoneHint: Used for nightly automation — Done operations are archived at midnight in this timezone.
preferences.loaderAnimationHint: The animated Myra mark shown next to the status message while an agent is running.
preferences.openAtLoginHint: Start Myra Agents automatically when you log in to your computer.

appUpdate.title: App updates
appUpdate.description: Update Myra Agents to the latest version, installed and verified automatically.
appUpdate.checkFailed: Couldn't check for updates. Please try again later.

data.title: Data Management
data.exportDescription: Download the current board as a JSON backup.
data.importDescription: Restore tasks from a previously exported JSON file.
data.clearHistoryDescription: Remove all past agent run records, and clear Done cards off the Operations board.
data.clearHistoryConfirmDescription: All run records will be permanently deleted and every Done card removed from the Operations board. This action cannot be undone.
data.clearLogsDescription: Remove stored execution logs after confirmation.
data.clearLogsConfirm: Clear all logs? This action cannot be undone.

upsell.title: Myra Pro
upsell.body: Myra Pro lets you reach your agents from anywhere. The free app is desktop-only — upgrade to use the web app and observe your machines remotely.
upsell.signInTitle: Already Pro? Sign in to your hub

local.dialogDescription: Run coding agents against a model on this machine — no cloud, no API key.
local.updateHint: Your Ollama is too old to launch coding agents. Update it to continue.
local.updateAvailable: A new Ollama version is available — you have {current}, latest is {latest}.
local.ready: Ollama is ready — pull a model below.
local.versionTooOld: Ollama {version} · update needed for local agents
local.panelDescription: Install Ollama, run its daemon, and pull models you can run agents against locally.

agents.installGateDescription: Install it to configure and run this agent.
agents.installDescription: Run one of these commands in a terminal, or use the one-click install. Then restart the agent check.
agents.effortNoVariants: This model has no effort variants

suggestion.title: Change requested during a run
suggestion.hint: You asked for changes on an operation of this patrol. If the agent keeps needing the same correction, fold it into the instruction so future runs get it right the first time.

scheduleEdit.discardDescription: Your unsaved changes will be lost and won't be applied.
scheduleEdit.activateDescription: This patrol is inactive. Activate it now so it runs on its trigger, or add it inactive and enable it later.
scheduleEdit.runAgainDescription: This patrol already has a run in progress. Starting another one now will run it a second time in parallel.
scheduleEdit.subtitle: Deploy recurring patrols with AI agents triggered by events.
scheduleEdit.instructionPlaceholder: Describe what the agent should do on each run…
scheduleEdit.footer: Showing {shown} of {total} - Click to see all patrols

toast.needPrompt: Add an agent instruction before saving.

scheduleEdit.oneScheduledOnly: Only one scheduled trigger is allowed. Remove the existing one to add another.
scheduleEdit.noFoldersYet: No folders yet — add one below
scheduleEdit.folderRequired: Add a folder for this patrol to run in.
scheduleEdit.branchNotGit: This folder isn't a git repository.
scheduleEdit.deleteColorDescription: Used by: {patrols}. Tags will revert to their default color.
scheduleEdit.createTagHint: Type a name to create a new tag

welcome.title: Welcome to Myra Agents
welcome.description: Myra Agents is a platform for automating the repetitive work you do. Create and monitor all your Myra agents in one place — local-first and open-source.

agent.title: Your agent is ready
agent.description: Myra ships with a built-in agent — no install, no CLI to set up.
agent.name: Myra agent
agent.cardDescription: Runs on the Myra hub out of the box, or with your own API key.
agent.footnote: Prefer your own CLI? Add opencode, Claude, Copilot, or a custom binary anytime in Settings.

connect.title: Connect a model
connect.description: The built-in agent runs on an LLM. Use a cloud model via OpenRouter, or a local model via Ollama.
connect.gifAlt: Copying a model id from an OpenRouter model page
connect.gifCaption: Every model on <link>openrouter.ai/models</link> shows its id under the name — copy it and paste it above.

ollama.name: Ollama
ollama.installHint: Ollama runs local models, private and offline. Install it in one click.
ollama.hintPulled: Choose one of your pulled models, or type any tag.
ollama.hintPull: Type a model tag — pull it from Settings after Ollama is installed.

folder.title: Choose a working folder
folder.description: Agents run inside this folder by default — typically the project or repo you want them to work on.
folder.hint: You can override this per card and per patrol later. Leave it as your home folder to decide each time.

ready.title: You're all set
ready.description: Here's your setup. You can change any of it from Settings whenever you like.
ready.cta: Head to the board, add a card with a prompt, and launch your first run.
